user_query:
I need a production-ready TLS certificate audit across a small set of domains and ports. Read the host list from input/hosts.txt (one host per line; allow host:port and default to 443 when no port is specified). Also read policy settings from input/policy.yaml and, if present, a prior snapshot from input/previous_report.json.

Using a TLS inspection tool, retrieve for each host: subject, issuer, valid_from, valid_to, fingerprint_sha256 (lowercase hex, no colons), and subject_alt_names (array). Compute days_until_expiry (integer, based on valid_to vs now) and set expiring_soon to true if days_until_expiry is less than or equal to the threshold in policy.yaml. If inspection fails, still include the host with valid=false and an error message, and set certificate to null.

Requirements:
- Sort the host entries by days_until_expiry ascending; put any entries with valid=false at the end of the list (keep their order otherwise).
- Default to port 443 when not specified in the input file.
- Compare the new results against input/previous_report.json if it exists and identify any hosts whose fingerprint_sha256 has changed, and any hosts that are newly added or removed compared to the previous file.
- Write a machine-readable JSON report to output/report.json with this structure:
  {
    "generated_at": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
    "policy": { "expiring_soon_days": <int> },
    "hosts": [
      {
        "host": "<host string from input>",
        "valid": <bool>,
        "expiring_soon": <bool>,
        "days_until_expiry": <int>,
        "error": "<string or empty if none>",
        "certificate": {
          "subject": "<string>",
          "issuer": "<string>",
          "valid_from": "<string>",
          "valid_to": "<string>",
          "fingerprint_sha256": "<64-char lowercase hex no colons>",
          "subject_alt_names": ["<dns1>", "<dns2>", ...]
        }
      },
      ...
    ]
  }
- Create a human-friendly summary at output/summary.md that includes:
  1) A top section with counts: Total hosts, Expiring soon count.
  2) A markdown table with columns: Host | Valid | DaysUntilExpiry | ExpiringSoon
  3) A section titled "## Comparison Against Previous" listing:
     - "Fingerprints changed:" followed by a comma-separated list or "None"
     - "Added hosts:" followed by a list or "None"
     - "Removed hosts:" followed by a list or "None"

Please write all artifacts only under output/ and do not hardcode absolute paths. If any hosts fail to inspect, keep going and include them as failed records with clear errors.
